revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:人工温泉]]



*{{ill|Constructed Hot Spring|ja|造成温泉|1=Constructed Hot Spring|2=ja|3=造成温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constructed Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Constructed Hot Spring|qq=}}
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:21, 25 March 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}
'''Artificial Hot Spring''' (人工温泉) refers to a general term for bathing facilities and related goods that use chemical agents and {{ill|Ore|en|Ore|qid=Q102798|lt=mineral ore|1=Ore}} which meet the {{ill|Conditions|ja|条件|lt=conditions described below|lt_ja=以下に示す条件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conditions|1=condition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11270894}}.
== Conditions ==
* Must possess {{ill|Medicinal efficacy|ja|薬効|lt=Medicinal efficacy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medicinal efficacy|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 医薬品の薬効分類|1=medication classification|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2943035}} and be capable of being labeled or posted.
* Its medicinal efficacy must derive from {{ill|Mineral (nutrient)|en|Mineral (nutrient)|qid=Q189598|lt=Minerals|1=Mineral (nutrient)}} originating from natural {{ill|Mineral|en|Mineral|qid=Q7946|lt=Minerals|1=Mineral}}, as in {{ill|Natural Hot Spring|ja|天然温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Hot Spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}}.
* Must produce medicinal efficacy by being mechanically introduced or installed.
Therefore, the chemical agents and mineral ores used must be quasi-drugs ({{ill|Quasi-drug|ja|医薬部外品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quasi-drug|1=quasi-drug|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404705}})<ref group="注">Quasi-drugs refer to products that are manufactured and sold under strict controls in facilities with manufacturing approval and quasi-drug manufacturing licenses, possessing relatively mild medicinal efficacy and capable of being labeled or posted.</ref> obtained directly from natural minerals.
There are examples such as those in which {{ill|Carbon dioxide|en|Carbon dioxide|qid=Q1997|lt=Carbon dioxide|1=Carbon dioxide}} packed in a {{ill|Gas cylinder|en|Gas cylinder|qid=Q774332|lt=Gas cylinder|1=Gas cylinder}} is dissolved in the bath water<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/product/sparklingcarbo-sc401.html |title=スパークリングカーボ SC401 |publisher=三菱ケミカルアクア･ソリューションズ |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref>, or ones in which mineral ores are placed in bathtubs or circulation equipment for immersion<ref name="komeisekionsen">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://komeisekionsen.com/syouhin/system.htm |title=光明石温泉 製品システム |publisher=長原産業 |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref>. For example, in the former case, there is the {{ill|Artificial Carbonated Spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉#人工の二酸化炭素泉|lt=Artificial Carbonated Spring|lt_ja=人工炭酸泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Artificial Carbonated Spring|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371830}} developed in 1997 by the current {{ill|Mitsubishi Chemical Holdings Group|ja|三菱ケミカルホールディングスグループ|lt=Mitsubishi Chemical Aqua Solutions|lt_ja=三菱ケミカルアクア･ソリューションズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsubishi Chemical Holdings Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三菱ケミカルグループ|1=Mitsubishi Chemical Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3107966}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/history/ |title=炭酸泉の歴史 |publisher=三菱ケミカルアクア･ソリューションズ |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref>, while in the latter case, mineral ore known as “Kōmeiseki” mined at Abe Mine in Okayama Prefecture by Kōmeiseki Seizōsho is used<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://koumeiseki.co.jp/ |title=株式会社光明石製造所 |publisher=光明石製造所 |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref><ref name="komeisekionsen" />. Artificial Carbonated Spring may also be applied to natural hot springs depending on the spring water quality<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/faq/ |title=炭酸泉についてのQ&A |publisher=三菱ケミカルアクア･ソリューションズ |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref>. Also, Le Furo’s {{ill|Craft Onsen|ja|クラフト温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Craft Onsen|1=Craft Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109594718}} is a product that enhances natural hot spring water by means of mineral ores<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://le-furo.com/craftonsen/ |title=クラフト温泉について |publisher=Le Furo |accessdate=2021-09-12 |qq=}}</ref>.
== Artificial Hot Springs Using Mineral Ores ==
In Japan, many products are sold that claim to provide an effect equivalent to {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} by allowing mineral ores (or processed products) containing {{ill|Radon|en|Radon|qid=Q1133|lt=Radon|1=Radon}} and {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|qid=Q1128|lt=Radium|1=Radium}}{{ill|Chemical element|en|Chemical element|qid=Q11344|lt=Element|1=Chemical element}} to permeate household bathtubs, but tests conducted by the {{ill|Consumer Affairs Center|ja|国民生活センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Consumer Affairs Center|1=National Consumer Affairs Center of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421137}} have indicated that in some cases the leaching concentrations of radon and radium are low, and the expected effects cannot be achieved<ref>[http://www.kokusen.go.jp/test/data/s_test/n-20100421_1.html A product that claimed to become a radon and radium hot spring simply by taking a bath] ({{ill|Consumer Affairs Center|ja|国民生活センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Consumer Affairs Center|1=National Consumer Affairs Center of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421137}} {{ill|2010|en|2010|qid=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} {{ill|April 21|en|April 21|qid=Q2525|lt=April 21|1=April 21}} announcement)</ref>.
== Footnotes ==

=== Notes ===
<references group="注"/>
=== Sources ===
<references/>
== Related Topics ==

* {{ill|Helston|ja|ヘルストン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helston|1=Helston|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11336643}}
* {{ill|Pseudoscience|en|Pseudoscience|qid=Q483677|lt=Pseudoscience|1=Pseudoscience}}
* {{ill|Unfair business practices|en|Unfair business practices|qid=Q11791804|lt=Fraudulent Business Practices|1=Unfair business practices}}
